tldr: Affected versions of `wolfssl` are vulnerable to improper input validation in TLS 1.3 CertificateVerify signature algorithm negotiation, allowing a downgrade of the negotiated signature algorithm. A malicious peer can cause the connection to proceed with a weaker algorithm (for example ECDSA P256) even when a stronger algorithm (such as ECDSA P521) was originally offered by the client, reducing the intended security level.
how_to_fix: Upgrade the `wolfSSL.wolfssl` library to the patch version.
